answer.text: Government income and expenditure in equilibrium with no major changes needed
Central bank maintaining stable interest rates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Neutral fiscal policy = government income and expenditure are in equilibrium. No major changes required in fiscal policies. **The Trap:** Large deficits = expansionary. Aggressive spending = expansionary. Stable rates = monetary policy, not fiscal. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fiscal policy stance tested in NISM Series XV and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Neutral fiscal policy is rare in practice. Most governments run deficits (expansionary) or occasionally surpluses (contractionary).
